title: Franklin County Domestic and Juvenile Court

communications.subject: Ohio Open Records Law Request: Franklin County Domestic and Juvenile Court
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Ohio Open Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

I request the following records for the period January 1 2026, through  March 1, 2026:

All electronic communications — including email, Microsoft Teams messages (private chats, group chats, and channel messages), and any attachments thereto  sent from or received by the user accounts of Judge Lasheyl Stroud, her judicial staff attorney, her bailiff, and any judicial assistant, that reference, discuss, request, grant, deny, or otherwise concern the continuance or rescheduling of any matter on a Judge Lasheyl Stroud's docket.

All electronic communications — including email, Microsoft Teams messages, and any attachments thereto  sent from the user account of Aeriol Scruggs, bailiff to Judge Lasheyl Stroud, to any attorney of record in any matter pending before Judge Stroud, that reference, discuss, communicate, or otherwise concern Judge Stroud's position regarding the continuance, rescheduling, postponement, or adjournment of any matter on her docket.

This request includes communications:
a. directed to private counsel, public defenders, prosecuting attorneys, guardians ad litem, and court-appointed special advocates;
b. sent on Judge Stroud's behalf, at her direction, or otherwise conveying her stated position on continuances;
c. responsive to attorney inquiries or continuance motions, whether the response was sent before or after a formal motion was filed.

This request includes any responsive communications subsequently deleted but recoverable through Microsoft Purview compliance content search, including from the Deleted Items folder, the Recoverable Items folder, or substrate retention.

For purposes of this request, "continuance" includes any postponement, adjournment, rescheduling, reset, or extension of a previously set hearing, trial, or other proceeding, however captioned in the communication.

Any redactions must be accompanied by a statement of the specific statutory basis claimed for each redaction.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

John Doe

title: Franklin County Domestic and Juvenile Court

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Ohio Open Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

I request the following records for the period March 10, 2026 through May 15, 2026, concerning the user account assigned to Judge Lasheyl Stroud and any judicial assistant, bailiff, or chambers staff account associated with her chambers:


1.  Microsoft Purview unified audit log entries for the listed accounts, exported in their native CSV or JSON format from the Microsoft Purview compliance portal (compliance.microsoft.com), without summarization or filtering. This includes audit records of, at minimum, the following categories of activity:

a. Email activity (Outlook / Exchange Online):

All messages sent and received
All messages deleted, whether moved to the Deleted Items folder, removed from Deleted Items, or permanently purged from the Recoverable Items folder
All messages opened or accessed
Creation, modification, or removal of mailbox rules (including auto-delete, auto-forward, or auto-move rules)
Any export of mailbox contents to a separate file (such as a PST export)
Any changes to mailbox settings, including the granting or removal of permissions allowing other users to access the mailbox

b. Microsoft Teams activity:

All messages sent in private chats, group chats, and channels
All messages deleted from any chat or channel
All messages edited, including the prior content of edited messages where retained
All chat content accessed or retrieved by the user (including scrolling back through prior conversations)
All Teams meetings attended or organized, including attendee lists, duration, and any associated recordings or transcripts
Any deletion of entire channels or removal of members from channels or teams
Any changes to channel or team settings
Any apps installed or removed from the user's Teams environment
Any access to attachments, images, or files shared within chats

c. File activity (SharePoint and OneDrive):

All files and folders accessed, downloaded, modified, uploaded, renamed, moved, or copied
All files and folders deleted, including from the first-stage recycle bin and the second-stage recycle bin
All deletions of file version history
All changes to file or folder sharing, including the creation or removal of sharing links, anonymous links, and access permissions

d. Compliance, retention, and eDiscovery activity:

Any content search created, started, or exported through the Microsoft Purview compliance portal
Any eDiscovery case opened
Any litigation hold or retention hold created, modified, or removed for the account or its associated mailbox or storage
Any retention policy created, modified, or removed that affects the account

e. Account and authentication activity:

All sign-in events, successful and failed, including timestamp, source IP address, and device used
Any password resets
Any changes to multi-factor authentication configuration
Any changes to user account settings or group memberships

2. Microsoft Teams chat and channel message contents for the listed accounts during the time period, including 1:1 chats, group chats, and channel messages, and including any messages subsequently deleted but recoverable through the Teams compliance content search or substrate retention. To the extent any message has been edited, please provide all versions retained.

3.Microsoft Teams meeting records for any meeting attended or organized by the listed accounts during the time period, including attendee lists, meeting recordings, transcripts, and chat from the meeting.
Exchange Online Message Trace records for inbound and outbound email involving the listed accounts during the time period.

4. Exchange Online Message Trace records for inbound and outbound email involving the listed accounts during the time period.

5.Microsoft Purview eDiscovery activity records showing any searches, holds, or exports affecting the listed accounts or their mailboxes/sites during the time period or in the 30 days preceding it.

6. Mailbox and OneDrive retention hold status records for the listed accounts, including any litigation hold, retention policy, or in-place hold applied to or removed from those accounts at any time in 2026.

7. Microsoft Intune or endpoint management records for any device assigned to the listed users, including device check-ins, compliance status changes, app deployment events, remote wipe events, retire events, and any selective wipe of corporate data.

8. Windows Security event logs from any workstation or laptop assigned to the listed users for the time period, including logon, logoff, account lockout, and removable-storage events.

9. All IT helpdesk tickets, internal IT email or Teams chats, and other written communications to or from any employee of the court or county information technology functions concerning the listed accounts, mailboxes, or devices during the period February 1, 2026 through April 15, 2026.

10. Backup, restore, and archive job logs for the listed accounts during the time period, including any successful or attempted restore from backup and any change to backup retention.

11. Document destruction logs, shredding records, and certificates of destruction generated during the time period referencing Judge Stroud, her chambers, or her case files.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

John Doe
